title: 7 Travel Stocks to Buy Right Now
text: As the Covid-19 crisis initially capsized global societies, many daring investors targeted travel stocks to buy for the long haul. Their thinking centered on the eventual comeback effect.

title: Cruising Toward a Better Future, Royal Caribbean Group Outlines Progress in Annual Seastainability Report
text: Expanding on 15 years of sustainability reporting, the company, also releases its first Task Force on Climate-Related Financial Disclosures Report  MIAMI , April 20, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Royal Caribbean Group (NYSE: RCL) released its 15th annual Seastainability Report today, providing an in-depth update on the company's corporate responsibility strategy and performance across its three wholly owned brands – Royal Caribbean International, Celebrity Cruises and Silversea Cruises. 2022 marked a watershed moment in the company's advancement of Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) efforts.

title: Better Buy in 2023: Carnival vs. Royal Caribbean Stock
text: Carnival Corporation's capacity is now 4.5% higher than in 2019. Royal Caribbean more than doubled its revenue last quarter.

title: Better Buy: Carnival Corporation vs. Royal Caribbean Cruises
text: Carnival and Royal Caribbean both survived the COVID-19 crisis. But both companies took on a lot more debt to stay solvent.

title: Companies Are Dropping Like Flies (And 10 Are Most At Risk Now)
text: Bankruptcy is a real and rising risk again — retraining investors to look for warning signs a company is about to collapse. Failing to spot a bankruptcy can be catastrophic.

title: Celebrity Cruises' President and CEO Lisa Lutoff-Perlo Transitions to Vice Chair, External Affairs; Laura Hodges Bethge Named to Succeed Her
text: MIAMI , April 6, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Today, Royal Caribbean Group (NYSE: RCL) announced that after an illustrious tenure at the helm of Celebrity Cruises, Lisa Lutoff-Perlo will transition to Vice Chair, External Affairs for Royal Caribbean Group, effective May 1. In her new role, Lutoff-Perlo will continue reporting to Royal Caribbean Group President and CEO Jason Liberty.

title: Are Consumer Discretionary Stocks Lagging Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL) This Year?
text: Here is how Royal Caribbean (RCL) and Wynn Resorts (WYNN) have performed compared to their sector so far this year.
